keys.description: spawn backend: docker (host socket API), k8s (Pods via kubectl under the runtime's RBAC), process (child processes — lite)
uvicorn bind host
uvicorn bind port
uvicorn/app log level
backing for the durable schedule.v1 cron store; unset ⇒ no scheduler is constructed
scheduler tick-loop interval (s)
k8s backend namespace, injected via the downward API
docker socket/endpoint override for the docker backend
network spawned workloads join — compose sets the project network so bots resolve meeting-api/redis by service name
shm-size for spawned browser-bot containers
grace (s) a stopping workload gets between SIGTERM and force-kill

keys.default: (chart's global.tolerations, JSON — the runtime's own tolerations)
keys.description: k8s backend: JSON array of toleration objects stamped onto every SPAWNED Pod's spec so a bare `kubectl run` bot/agent Pod (not a Deployment child) tolerates the same taints the runtime does — else it strands Pending forever on an all-tainted pool (#673). Empty [] ⇒ no tolerations; malformed JSON is fatal at spawn (a silently dropped constraint is the bug)

keys.default: (chart's global.nodeSelector, JSON — the runtime's own nodeSelector)
keys.description: k8s backend: JSON object of node-label selectors stamped onto every SPAWNED Pod's spec so a bare `kubectl run` bot/agent Pod schedules onto the same pool the runtime does. Empty {} ⇒ no selector; malformed JSON is fatal at spawn (#673)
the meeting-bot image the `meeting-bot` profile spawns (vexaai/vexa-bot:v012)
active-phase remote-audio silence window before completed(left_alone); forwarded to spawned bots when set
minimum Google Meet speaker audio window before transcription submission; forwarded to spawned bots
Google Meet speaker-stream submission interval; forwarded to spawned bots
consecutive transcription matches required to confirm a Google Meet segment; forwarded to spawned bots
maximum Google Meet speaker audio buffer before force flush; forwarded to spawned bots
Google Meet speaker idle flush timeout; forwarded to spawned bots
the agent control-plane image tag the worker image is derived from (worker_image_for); unset ⇒ the `agent` profile cannot spawn

keys.default: (derived from AGENT_IMAGE by worker_image_for; ensured/pulled at boot on the docker backend)
keys.description: the per-dispatch WORKER image (claude-in-container) the runtime spawns for `agent` workloads
process-backend override: the executable that IS the meeting-bot (lite runs /usr/local/bin/vexa-bot-launch)
process-backend override: the executable that IS the agent worker (lite runs /usr/local/bin/vexa-agent-worker)

DOCKER-HOST path (NOT an in-container path) to a claude credentials JSON (~/.claude/.credentials.json); the docker backend bind-mounts it READ-ONLY into every spawned agent-worker at /root/.claude/.credentials.json — the claude-code runner's subscription credential path. compose also mirror-mounts it into the runtime container at /var/lib/vexa/host-claude-credentials so the config.v1 file probe can verify the host file actually exists (docker turns a MISSING host path into an empty directory in the worker → 'Model inference failed: Not logged in')
API-key credential path, brokered into spawned workers (alternative to the subscription mount)
OAuth/OpenRouter-style bearer credential path, brokered into spawned workers (pairs with ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL)
provider-agnostic llm-module credential path, brokered into spawned workers (CompletionPort adapters)
endpoint the ANTHROPIC_* credentials authenticate against, brokered into spawned workers
model id brokered into spawned workers
opus-alias model id brokered into spawned workers
sonnet-alias model id brokered into spawned workers
haiku-alias model id brokered into spawned workers
llm-module CompletionPort adapter key, brokered into spawned workers
llm-module endpoint, brokered into spawned workers
llm-module deployment-default model, brokered into spawned workers
llm-module completion cap, brokered into spawned workers
the agent HARNESS for workspace turns, brokered into spawned workers

keys.default: (unset — the worker runs its baked-in source)
keys.description: dev loop: host path of the agent source bind-mounted over the worker's /app
process-backend workload log dir — per-workload stdout/stderr capture (the process analog of docker logs); fail-open to DEVNULL if unwritable

scheduler.description: the durable schedule.v1 cron (routine jobs, join-retry re-arms) is live
scheduler.when_unconfigured: /schedule answers a typed 503 (honest absence); one-shot workload spawns are unaffected

bot_spawn.description: the `meeting-bot` profile can spawn (the meetings plane's bot container)
bot_spawn.when_unconfigured: a runtime.v1 create with profile=meeting-bot fails loud at spawn time (no image to run)

agent_spawn.description: the `agent` profile can spawn (the per-dispatch claude-in-container worker)
agent_spawn.when_unconfigured: a runtime.v1 create with profile=agent fails loud at spawn time (no worker image derivable)

model_inference.description: spawned agent-workers get WORKING model-provider credentials brokered in — either the mounted claude subscription file (HOST_CLAUDE_CREDENTIALS) or an API-style key (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN / VEXA_LLM_API_KEY)
model_inference.when_unconfigured: agent dispatches spawn but every turn fails inside the worker ('Model inference failed: Not logged in' / provider 401) — configure ONE credential path

surface_only.reason: set on the runtime by compose/helm/lite but never read by runtime code (the internal-call secret is a gateway↔admin-api↔meeting-api concern) — documented drift surfaced by gate:config-contract; candidate for a deploy-surface cleanup
